title: Engine-aware dependency resolution (localDeps/remoteDeps) — fix the half-wired engine split; status gate ignores it
description:
BUG (found live 2026-06-30, A100 Pod, fresh GGUF-only volume): LTX shows INSTALLED on the Models page but the PROMPT BOX is HIDDEN and generation is blocked. Root: the bf16-local/GGUF-Pod split (MPI-157) is HALF-WIRED. The `engine: 'local'|'remote'` tag on individual DEPS entries + `filterDepsByEngine` correctly filters the DOWNLOAD path (bf16 was correctly NOT downloaded to the Pod) and the syncModelInstalled PAYLOAD — but the STATUS GATE does NOT apply the filter. isModelUsable / isOperationInstalled (modelRegistry.js ~246-282) walk deriveInstalledOps over resolveFullUniverse(model), which is ENGINE-AGNOSTIC BY DESIGN (returns ALL deps incl ltx23-transformer-bf16). On the Pod, bf16 is legitimately absent -> gate sees a missing dep -> model 'not fully usable' -> no prompt box. It WORKED on the old volume only because that volume (pre-split) had BOTH transformers present. So a fresh, correctly-split install is BROKEN.

WHY THE PER-DEP TAG IS THE WRONG PRIMITIVE (user, across the session): the `engine` tag forces EVERY consumer (download start, Pod install, status check, install trigger, status GATE, installed badge, prompt-box gate, shared-dep protection) to REMEMBER to filter. Miss one (the gate did) and you get exactly this bug. It's also 3 mechanisms for one concept (engine tag on weight + untagged node that installs everywhere + ggufWhenRemote bool for the workflow). Not scalable-foundation.

FIX (user's design, agreed) — make the engine-correct set the DEFAULT at the RESOLUTION layer so no consumer can half-wire it. Model declares structurally:
  dependencies: [...shared...]                       // both engines (VAE, CLIP, LoRAs, projection)
  localDeps:  ['ltx23-transformer-bf16']             // local-only additions
  remoteDeps: ['ltx23-transformer-gguf', 'ComfyUI-GGUF'] // remote-only additions (the GGUF node belongs here too — it's Pod-only, currently UNTAGGED so it installs locally for nothing)
Shared deps listed ONCE (not duplicated). Resolution = dependencies + (localDeps XOR remoteDeps by engine). DROP the per-DEPS `engine` tag entirely — the model structure carries it. resolveDeps/resolveFullUniverse gain an `engine` param; resolveFullUniverse-for-shared-dep-protection still unions BOTH engine sets (a dep shared across models/engines must not be deleted). Every consumer passes its engine and gets the right set automatically: download (already engine-aware via isRemote), Pod install (remote), the STATUS GATE (the missed one — isModelUsable/isOperationInstalled must resolve the engine-correct universe via the current engine), the badge, the prompt box.

THIS ALSO FIXES (the consistency wins): (1) ComfyUI-GGUF node currently installs LOCALLY for no reason (no engine tag) — move to remoteDeps -> Pod-only. (2) Replace the GGUF-specific `ggufWhenRemote` boolean + _toGgufFilename string-surgery with a generic per-model workflows.local / workflows.remote map (or keep the suffix but derive from engine, not a GGUF-named flag). (3) Future case 'model X needs node N on Pod only' = add N to X.remoteDeps, done — the general version of GGUF.

FILES: js/data/modelConstants/models.js (LTX entry: split dependencies into dependencies+localDeps+remoteDeps; drop ggufWhenRemote -> workflows.local/remote); js/data/modelConstants/dependencies.js (drop the `engine` field on the two transformers + add it conceptually via the model lists; keep both dep entries); js/data/modelConstants/resolveModelDeps.js (engine-param on resolveDeps/resolveFullUniverse; keep an all-engines union for shared-dep protection; DROP/retarget filterDepsByEngine — callers pass engine to the resolver instead of post-filtering); js/data/modelRegistry.js (syncModelInstalled + isModelUsable + isOperationInstalled + deriveInstalledOps consumers must resolve the engine-correct universe by the CURRENT engine, remoteEngineClient.isRemote()); routes/downloadManager.js + routes/remoteModels.js (the server boundaries — resolve by engine instead of filterDepsByEngine post-filter); js/components/.../MpiModelManager _install. Update tests/resolve-model-deps.test.cjs.

VERIFY: on a fresh GGUF-only Pod volume, LTX shows installed AND the prompt box appears AND a gen runs. Locally (bf16 only) same. Shared-dep protection still keeps a dep another model needs. 10/10+ resolver tests pass with the engine-aware resolution.

CURRENT STATE = BROKEN on a fresh Pod (GGUF-only volume) until this lands — the shipped MPI-157 split is download-correct but gate-incorrect. INTERIM UNBLOCK for testing: install BOTH transformers on the volume (old behavior) OR temporarily revert the model deps to a single shared transformer. BRANCH: RunPod. Relates: MPI-157 (the split this completes), the architecture pushback this session.
